title: Extract the dev-pipeline skeleton as a config-parameterised template and update the CLI scaffold
purpose: Lifts the scout→dev→verify→review→harden control skeleton out of brief_dev.gleam into a reusable, config-parameterised pipeline module so a new agentic family is a PipelineConfig (its prompts, its stage schemas, its gate commands) plus its prompts/schemas — not a from-scratch workflow body (RM-023, S1). With WT-001's thin facts/sealed-payload flow and WT-002's imported harness in place, the only bespoke thing left per family is the loop body itself; this brief makes that shared. brief_dev is then re-expressed as a config over the skeleton, and the dev_pipeline CLI template (shipped by BD-007) is updated to scaffold the thin harness+pipeline shape so `aion new` produces the cheap pattern by default (S4). The agent step is parameterised, and the norn driver stays worker-side (ADR-011, S14). This is the last brief in the cluster: it lands the whole reshape clean.
task: Extract the control skeleton into src/stacked_dev/pipeline.gleam parameterised by a PipelineConfig (src/stacked_dev/pipeline_config.gleam), re-express brief_dev as a config over it, and update the dev_pipeline template. SCOPE IN: the PipelineConfig type (stage prompt templates, stage schemas, gate command set, the required caps/backoff), the shared pipeline body that drives scout→dev→verify→review→harden from a config, brief_dev.gleam reduced to constructing its config and calling the skeleton, pipeline_test.gleam proving the same flow runs from brief_dev's config AND from a second distinct config, and the crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/ update to the thin shape. SCOPE OUT: the facts/sealed-payload flow (WT-001) and the worker-harness (WT-002) are already in place and unchanged; the outer stacked_dev arc and the dispatch/gate workflows are untouched. DEPENDENCY: serial after WT-001 and WT-002 (CN10). STORY SPLITS: S11 (named reusable modules) is shared with WT-002 — WT-002 delivers the harness module, this brief the pipeline-skeleton module; both complete the outcome. S14 (norn driver stays worker-side, aion_kit cross-cutting only) is shared with WT-001 — WT-001 keeps render worker-side respecting ADR-011, this brief parameterises the agent step through config hooks so the template never bakes the norn runtime in. THE PARAMETERISATION RULE (CN7, C23): every bound is a config or input field — PipelineConfig carries no default cap, backoff, deadline, or timeout, and the skeleton imposes none of its own (ADR-001 spirit, brief-dev CN2). THE AGENT-STEP RULE (CN8, ADR-011, S14): the skeleton parameterises the agent invocation through the config; it imports no norn-specific runtime, and the norn driver stays worker-side. THE NO-REGRESSION RULE (P3, P5): re-expressing brief_dev as a config must drive byte-identically the same stage flow, the same seven status phases, the same six-variant error taxonomy, and the same facts-routing WT-001 landed — pipeline_test proves the brief_dev config reproduces the brief_dev behaviour. THE PROOF-OF-REUSE RULE (C24, S1): a second distinct config in pipeline_test exercises the skeleton without re-authoring the body, demonstrating a new family is configuration.

requirements.title: The PipelineConfig type
requirements.spec: Create src/stacked_dev/pipeline_config.gleam defining PipelineConfig — the family's configuration surface. It SHALL carry: the four stage render entrypoints (scout/dev/review/resume prompt builders, the worker-side render functions the agent step calls), the three stage schemas (scout/dev/review report schemas the agent runs are constrained to), the gate command set (the scoped-check and full-check harness entrypoints), and the required loop bounds (verify_fix_cap, round_backoff_ms) threaded from the workflow input. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT bake any default cap, backoff, deadline, or timeout into PipelineConfig — every bound is a field supplied by the family or the input (CN7, C23). THE SYSTEM SHALL parameterise the agent step through config-supplied entrypoints, and pipeline_config.gleam SHALL NOT import a norn-specific runtime (ADR-011, CN8): the norn driver stays worker-side and is reached only through the config's render/agent hooks.
acceptance: pipeline_config.gleam defines PipelineConfig with fields for the stage render entrypoints, the stage schemas, the gate command set, and the required verify_fix_cap/round_backoff_ms — and no default-valued bound.
grep for any literal cap/backoff/deadline/timeout default in pipeline_config.gleam returns nothing — every bound is a field.
grep for a norn-specific runtime import in pipeline_config.gleam returns nothing (the agent step is a config hook, ADR-011).
The config's gate command set references the WT-002 harness entrypoints, not a re-implementation.

requirements.title: The shared pipeline skeleton
requirements.spec: Create src/stacked_dev/pipeline.gleam holding the scout→dev→verify→review→harden control skeleton, parameterised by a PipelineConfig and the workflow input. WHEN the skeleton runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL drive the same flow brief_dev landed under WT-001: scout (status scouting), concurrent warm_build+dev (status developing) via the startup envelope, fail DevBlocked on the dev facts' blocked ids, the bounded verify-fix loop (verifying/fixing) over the dev facts' changed files with the config's verify_fix_cap and round_backoff_ms, dev_review (reviewing), fail ReviewDrifted on the review facts' drifted set (before the harden re-verify), the has-fixes harden re-run (hardening) failing HardenRegressed on regression, and converged. The skeleton SHALL thread sealed payloads and read only facts (WT-001's CN2 flow, unchanged), SHALL register exactly the seven status phases, and SHALL surface exactly the six BriefDevError variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT impose any bound beyond the config's required fields, and SHALL NOT import a rendering module into workflow code (rendering is the config's worker-side hooks, CN3).
acceptance: pipeline.gleam drives scout→dev→verify→review→harden from a PipelineConfig; the seven status phases (scouting, developing, verifying, fixing, reviewing, hardening, converged) are registered in order.
The skeleton reads only facts (DevFacts/ReviewFacts) for routing and threads sealed payloads — grep shows no full-report field access in pipeline.gleam (CN2).
The skeleton evaluates ReviewDrifted before the has_fixes harden re-verify, matching the WT-001 ordering.
workflow.sleep appears with duration.milliseconds(config-or-input round_backoff_ms) and no other duration literal; no default bound exists in the module.
The six BriefDevError variants (ScoutFailed, DevBlocked, VerifyFixExhausted, ReviewDrifted, HardenRegressed, BriefDevStageFailed) are the exact error surface.

requirements.title: Re-express brief_dev as a config over the skeleton
requirements.spec: Reduce src/brief_dev.gleam to constructing its PipelineConfig (its scout/dev/review/resume render hooks, its three stage schemas, its harness-backed gate commands, the verify_fix_cap/round_backoff_ms from its input) and delegating execution to pipeline.execute. WHEN brief_dev runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce byte-identically the same result the WT-001 brief_dev produced for the same input — same stage flow, same facts routing, same status phases, same errors (P3, P5). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT re-author the loop body in brief_dev.gleam: the control flow lives in pipeline.gleam, and brief_dev is its config plus the workflow entry/definition glue (workflow_type, status_query_name, run, definition). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT change the brief_dev workflow_type, status_query_name, input/output codecs, or the BriefDevResult shape WT-001 landed — the family redeploys as a unit (brief-dev CN6) and the outer arc's spawn shape is immutable (CN1).
acceptance: brief_dev.gleam builds a PipelineConfig and calls pipeline.execute; the scout→dev→verify→review→harden loop body no longer lives inline in brief_dev.gleam.
brief_dev.workflow_type == "brief_dev" and status_query_name == "brief_dev_status" are unchanged; the input/output codecs and BriefDevResult shape are the WT-001 ones.
The hermetic suite's brief_dev stage outcomes (scout failure, dev block, verify exhaustion, review drift, harden regression, happy path) are unchanged in meaning after the re-expression.
grep shows brief_dev.gleam imports pipeline and pipeline_config and dispatches no activity the skeleton does not (scout, warm_build, dev, scoped_checks, dev_resume, dev_review).

requirements.title: Pipeline-skeleton tests proving reuse
requirements.spec: Create test/pipeline_test.gleam. WHEN the suite runs, THE SYSTEM SHALL drive the skeleton from brief_dev's own PipelineConfig and assert it reproduces the brief_dev stage flow, status phases, facts routing, and error taxonomy (the no-regression proof, P3). It SHALL ALSO drive the skeleton from a SECOND, distinct PipelineConfig (different render hooks and stage schemas, the same harness gate commands) and assert the same control flow runs without re-authoring the body (the proof-of-reuse, C24, S1) — demonstrating a new agentic family is configuration. THE SYSTEM SHALL run under the existing PATH-shim seam (not the network or the engine for the config-shape assertions) and SHALL NOT run concurrently with gleam builds in the same checkout (brief-dev CN9).
acceptance: pipeline_test.gleam drives the skeleton from brief_dev's config and asserts the seven status phases and the six error variants are reachable as in WT-001.
pipeline_test.gleam drives the skeleton from a second distinct config and asserts the same scout→dev→verify→review→harden flow runs — the body is not re-authored, only the config differs.
A drifted review facts result fails ReviewDrifted before the harden re-verify under both configs.
cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test runs pipeline_test and it passes.

requirements.title: Update the dev_pipeline CLI template to the thin harness+pipeline shape
requirements.spec: Update crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/ to scaffold the thin shape this cluster establishes: facts + sealed payloads in the workflow, worker-side render via the aion_kit template primitive, the imported worker-harness, and the config-driven pipeline skeleton — with {{name}} placeholders. WHEN a project is scaffolded from the template, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce a workflow that reads only facts and threads sealed payloads (no in-workflow report decode, no in-workflow render), a config-parameterised pipeline body, and a worker that imports the harness and renders prompts worker-side. The rendered worker's gates SHALL pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT scaffold the old in-workflow rendering shape (the pre-reshape template is replaced, not kept alongside, ADR-002), and SHALL NOT bake the norn driver into the scaffolded toolkit surface (ADR-011) — the agent step is a config hook.
acceptance: The dev_pipeline template's scaffolded workflow reads facts and threads sealed payloads (no report decode or prompt render in the workflow module), and its pipeline body is config-driven.
The scaffolded worker imports a harness module and renders prompts worker-side via the aion_kit template primitive.
Rendering the template and running its worker gates (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test) passes clean.
grep across the rendered template shows no in-workflow rendering path and no norn runtime baked into the toolkit surface.

requirements.title: Cluster-landing gate: full suite, drift gate, and clean lints
requirements.spec: Bring the whole workflow-toolkit cluster clean at its landing tip. WHEN this brief lands, THE SYSTEM SHALL pass: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; the full workspace test suite; the package gleam test suite (facts_codecs_test, render_test, harness_test, pipeline_test, and the updated aion_stacked_dev_test); aion codegen --check; and bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh (exit 0, no docs/design-system/ file touched, CN5). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT carry any #[allow]/#[expect]/unwrap/expect/panic/todo bypass and SHALL NOT leave any file over 500 code lines (split pipeline/config/render/harness as needed). This requirement is the cluster's clean-landing assertion; it owns no new source file of its own beyond confirming the suite is green.
acceptance: cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exit 0 at the landing commit.
The full workspace suite and cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test both pass, running facts_codecs_test, render_test, harness_test, pipeline_test, and the updated hermetic suite.
cargo run -p aion-cli -- codegen examples/stacked-dev --check exits 0 and bash examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh exits 0 with no docs/design-system/ file modified.
No source file under examples/stacked-dev/src or worker/src exceeds 500 code lines, and grep finds no #[allow]/#[expect]/unwrap_used-bypassing usage introduced by the cluster.

boundaries: SHALL NOT re-author the control flow per family: the scout→dev→verify→review→harden body lives once in pipeline.gleam; brief_dev and any new family supply a PipelineConfig.
SHALL NOT bake any default cap, backoff, deadline, or timeout into PipelineConfig or the skeleton — every bound is a config or input field (CN7, C23).
SHALL NOT lift the norn agent driver into aion_kit or the skeleton — it stays worker-side and is reached through config hooks (ADR-011, CN8, S14).
SHALL NOT change the facts/sealed-payload flow WT-001 landed or the worker-harness WT-002 extracted — this brief lifts the loop body only.
SHALL NOT alter any OUTER stacked-dev contract or the brief_dev workflow_type/status_query/codecs/result shape (CN1, brief-dev CN6).
SHALL NOT keep the old in-workflow rendering shape in the CLI template alongside the thin one — the template is replaced (ADR-002).
SHALL NOT touch dispatch.gleam, gate.gleam, assemble_wave, or any file under docs/design-system/.
verification: WT-001 and WT-002 have landed before this brief is dispatched (depends_on, CN10).
grep confirms the scout→dev→verify→review→harden body lives in pipeline.gleam and brief_dev.gleam constructs a PipelineConfig and calls pipeline.execute — the loop is not inline in brief_dev anymore.
grep for any default cap/backoff/deadline/timeout in pipeline_config.gleam and pipeline.gleam returns nothing; grep for a norn runtime import in those modules returns nothing (ADR-011).
pipeline_test runs the skeleton from brief_dev's config and from a second distinct config, both green — the proof a new family is configuration (S1, C24).
Render the dev_pipeline template and run its worker gates (fmt, clippy -D warnings, test): all pass, scaffolding the thin shape (C26).

cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam build && gleam test exit 0; cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, the workspace suite, aion codegen --check, and check-schema-drift.sh all pass clean (C29, C30).
No file over 500 code lines; no #[allow]/unwrap/expect/panic introduced; the WT-001 dogfood arc still runs after the skeleton extraction (the re-expression preserves brief_dev behaviour).
